_comment: Value-thesis search config (v1.8) — beats the 'title trap'. Authored by Claude in Stages 4-5, then consumed by scripts/expand_titles.py. Lives at data/<user>/title-search.json (gitignored). 'expanded_titles' are the LLM 'radius search' around each seed: a wide set of adjacent titles across company stages that all map to the same value thesis — they drive the SEARCH PLAN (recall). 'match_keywords' are short signal phrases used only by --attribute to group fetched jobs by archetype (robust to real-world title variety, e.g. 'quant' + 'quantitative'); if omitted, attribution falls back to the expanded titles. Prefer FUNCTION words over SECTOR words in match_keywords (e.g. 'quant' not 'fintech') so a sector-adjacent role like a fintech CMO isn't mis-tagged. Titles are search HINTS; the JD body + Stage-6 capability/value-fit decide what to keep (precision).
value_thesis: Find a revenue lever, model it, build it, and own the decision end-to-end.

archetypes.name: Revenue-Lever Owner
archetypes.note: Owns a P&L / monetization lever end-to-end; strategy + build, not pure analytics.

archetypes.name: 0-to-1 Data/Growth Leader
archetypes.note: Builds the data/growth function from scratch at an early-stage or scaling company.

archetypes.name: Founder's Right-Hand / BizOps
archetypes.note: Cross-functional, high-autonomy 'figure out how we make money' role next to the CEO.
seed_titles: Chief of Staff to the CEO
expanded_titles: Chief of Staff to the CEO
